{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/262839898?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/bugbunny-research", "id": 262839898, "login": "bugbunny-research", "node_id": "U_kgDOD6qeWg", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/bugbunny-research", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-46553", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 2.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:P"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Summary
The upload-by-URL path did not enforce `NC_ATTACHMENT_FIELD_SIZE` against either the remote file's advertised `Content-Length` or the decoded length of a `data:` URI, allowing an authenticated user to bypass the configured per-file size limit.

### Details
The attachments service now checks `NC_ATTACHMENT_FIELD_SIZE` against both the HEAD response's `content-length` and the decoded length of a `data:` URI body before fetching. The local storage plugin additionally sets `maxContentLength` on the axios download so a malicious server cannot stream past the limit.

### Impact
Authenticated users with upload permission could attach files larger than the operator-configured limit, defeating storage and bandwidth caps.

### Credit
This issue was reported by [@bugbunny-research](https://github.com/bugbunny-research).", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0024, "percentile": 0.1481}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-8rwr-f68v-cvw6", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-21T20:38:58Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8rwr-f68v-cvw6", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-8rwr-f68v-cvw6"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-46553"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-21T20:38:58Z", "references": ["https://github.com/nocodb/nocodb/security/advisories/GHSA-8rwr-f68v-cvw6", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8rwr-f68v-cvw6"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/nocodb/nocodb/security-advisories/GHSA-8rwr-f68v-cvw6", "severity": "low", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/nocodb/nocodb", "summary": "NocoDB: Attachment Size Limit Bypass via Upload-by-URL", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-21T20:38:59Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-8rwr-f68v-cvw6", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "nocodb"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.301.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: NocoDB: Attachment Size Limit Bypass via Upload-by-URL
